deep_dive.title: SAFETY SENTRY: Context-Aware Human Intervention via EXECUTE-ASK-REFUSE Routing
deep_dive.abstract: LLM agents act on real-world environments through tool calls, and a single misjudged action can cause irreversible harm. The standard safeguard is a guard model that labels each proposed action as safe or unsafe, but this binary view conflates two distinct decisions: whether the action is harmful in itself, and whether it is appropriate given the user's context. It also operates at the granularity of action categories rather than individual instances, producing routine interruptions that erode autonomy and train users to wave through the most consequential alerts. We reframe the problem as a per-instance three-way routing decision over {EXECUTE, ASK, REFUSE} and instantiate it with Safety Sentry, a lightweight guard model whose inference reduces to a single decoding call. A single decoding-time threshold lets one fixed checkpoint be re-positioned across deployments of differing risk tolerance without retraining. Safety Sentry outperforms a broad set of open-weight and frontier closed-source baselines on overall accuracy and safety-related recall, while controlling both directional error rates simultaneously.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: lightweight guard model for LLM action safety routing
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 用于LLM动作安全路由的轻量级防护模型
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 用于LLM动作安全路由的轻量级防护模型
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 用于LLM动作安全路由的轻量级防护模型
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Existing binary guard models for LLM agents conflate harm and context-appropriateness, causing alert fatigue. Safety Sentry reframes this as a three-way routing decision (EXECUTE, ASK, REFUSE) using a lightweight guard model with reduced inference cost.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有LLM智能体的二分类防护模型混淆了动作本身的有害性与上下文适宜性，导致频繁误报和用户疲劳。本文将安全防护重构为实例级的三路路由决策（执行、询问、拒绝），并提出Safety Sentry轻量级防护模型，其推理开销显著降低，在保障安全的同时提升了用户自主性。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有LLM智能体的二分类防护模型混淆了动作本身的有害性与上下文适宜性，导致频繁误报和用户疲劳。本文将安全防护重构为实例级的三路路由决策（执行、询问、拒绝），并提出Safety Sentry轻量级防护模型，其推理开销显著降低，在保障安全的同时提升了用户自主性。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: compact efficient guard model for LLM input and output safety classification

quick_skim.title: SingGuard-NSFA: Extensible Guardrails for Agentic AI via Generative Reasoning and Real-Time Classification
quick_skim.abstract: We present nsfaguard, a guardrail framework for securing agentic AI systems against operational threats, such as prompt injection, sensitive information extraction, malicious code requests, dangerous tool misuse, and resource exhaustion. We first introduce the NSFA taxonomy, which organizes 185 risk variants into a CIA-triad-grounded hierarchy and is cross-validated against three well-established OWASP guidelines. Based on this taxonomy, we construct a benchmark suite spanning 133 languages, comprising over 93K purpose-built samples targeting both user queries and agent responses, along with 3,435 cross-source samples adapted from five public agent-security datasets. To detect these operational threats in practice, we develop a dual-mode approach combining SFT-based generative reasoning for interpretable offline auditing with discriminative classification heads on the frozen backbone, enabling real-time detection at approximately 50,ms. We release four models with 0.8B, 2B, 4B, and 9B parameters, all achieving $\geq$94% F1 on purpose-built benchmarks and surpassing the strongest competing guardrails by 6 to 12 absolute points. On cross-source evaluation, the 9B model attains 91.29% F1 with a more balanced precision--recall trade-off. Moreover, ablation experiments show that classification heads can equip a guardrail with risk detection capabilities beyond its original scope and achieve state-of-the-art performance. These results demonstrate the extensibility of the approach and its generality as a plug-in enhancement.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: guardrail framework with real-time classification for securing agentic AI against prompt injection and threats
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 针对智能体AI的实时分类防护栏框架，防御提示注入等威胁
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 针对智能体AI的实时分类防护栏框架，防御提示注入等威胁
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 针对智能体AI的实时分类防护栏框架，防御提示注入等威胁
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Securing agentic AI against operational threats like prompt injection requires effective guardrails. SingGuard-NSFA introduces the NSFA taxonomy for 185 risk variants, constructs a 93K+ sample benchmark, and proposes a guardrail framework combining generative reasoning with real-time classification for both user queries and agent responses.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 智能体AI面临提示注入等操作威胁，需要有效防护栏。SingGuard-NSFA提出NSFA风险分类法组织185种风险变体，构建含9.3万样本的基准，并提出结合生成式推理与实时分类的防护栏框架，对用户查询和智能体响应进行安全检测，为LLM输入输出安全提供了可扩展的防护方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 智能体AI面临提示注入等操作威胁，需要有效防护栏。SingGuard-NSFA提出NSFA风险分类法组织185种风险变体，构建含9.3万样本的基准，并提出结合生成式推理与实时分类的防护栏框架，对用户查询和智能体响应进行安全检测，为LLM输入输出安全提供了可扩展的防护方案。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: compact efficient guard model for LLM input and output safety classification

quick_skim.title: Silent Failures in Quantized LLM Reasoning: A Taxonomy-Based Analysis of Hollow Convergence and Failure Mode Shifts
quick_skim.abstract: We show that post-training quantization can silently alter how large language models reason even when task accuracy is preserved. Using a six-category failure taxonomy validated by two independent human annotators (Cohen's $κ$ = 0.906), we classify 30,000 chain-of-thought outputs from five instruction-tuned LLMs (3B--14B parameters) across three quantization precisions (FP32, FP16, NF4) and four reasoning benchmarks. We find that while accuracy is robust across precisions (maximum 3.1 pp drop), Hollow Convergence (correct answers reached through incomplete or unverifiable reasoning) shows a significant size-dependent shift under NF4, dropping sharply for the two smallest models tested but remaining invariant for models at 12B parameters and above. This effect is also benchmark-specific: GSM8K is categorically immune while LogiQA and ARC-Challenge show the largest shifts. Furthermore, under NF4, Shortcut Collapse rises from 44% to 78% of wrong-answer failures in LLaMA 3.2-3B while Confidence Snowballing collapses from 15.8% to near zero, a qualitative shift invisible to accuracy metrics. Finally, we show Hollow Convergence cannot be reliably detected from surface-level text features (best F1 = 0.53), establishing it as a deployment-relevant failure mode that standard evaluation pipelines cannot catch.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: analysis of quantization effects on LLM reasoning failure modes across precisions
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 分析量化精度对LLM推理失败模式的影响
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 分析量化精度对LLM推理失败模式的影响
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 分析量化精度对LLM推理失败模式的影响
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Post-training quantization can silently alter LLM reasoning even when accuracy is preserved. This study classifies 30K CoT outputs from five LLMs across FP32, FP16, and NF4 precisions, revealing that Hollow Convergence shifts significantly under NF4 for smaller models, providing crucial insights for deploying quantized safety classifiers.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 训练后量化可能在保持准确率的情况下悄然改变LLM推理方式。该研究对五个LLM在三种量化精度下的三万条思维链输出进行分类，发现NF4量化下小模型的空洞收敛现象显著偏移，而大模型保持不变。这为部署量化低比特安全分类器提供了关键洞察，表明量化可能影响模型安全判断的可靠性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 训练后量化可能在保持准确率的情况下悄然改变LLM推理方式。该研究对五个LLM在三种量化精度下的三万条思维链输出进行分类，发现NF4量化下小模型的空洞收敛现象显著偏移，而大模型保持不变。这为部署量化低比特安全分类器提供了关键洞察，表明量化可能影响模型安全判断的可靠性。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: quantized low-bit safety classifier or guard model for LLM content moderation

quick_skim.title: When Words Are Safe But Actions Kill: Probing Physical Danger Beyond Text Safety in Hidden-State Risk Space
quick_skim.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) increasingly serve as high-level planners for embodied agents, where linguistically benign instructions can become unsafe once grounded in the physical world. We study whether this physically grounded danger is the same safety problem as ordinary text-level content danger. Through hidden-state direction analysis and random-split null tests, we show that content danger (CD) and physical danger (PD) form separable signals in LLM representations across Qwen2.5-3B/7B/14B/32B, Phi-3.5 and SmolLM2. Building on the CD/PD separability, we propose PRISM, a single-layer L2-regularized logistic probe over full hidden states. PRISM achieves 86.2--87.7\% accuracy on SafeAgentBench with 11.7--13.7\% FPR, while same-scale LLM judges over-block safe tasks at 24.7--39.0\% FPR. We further introduce PhysicalSafetyBench-1K (PSB-1K), a contrastive benchmark of 1{,}000 physical-risk pairs without direct harm keywords, to test whether methods detect physically grounded danger rather than explicit unsafe wording. On PSB-1K, PRISM reaches 99.6\% accuracy and 0.7\% FPR, whereas a Qwen2.5-3B judge rejects 67.8\% of safe tasks. PRISM also replicates on SafeText and EARBench, supporting hidden-state probing as a representation-level method for physical safety beyond text moderation.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Single-layer probe for LLM safety classification
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 用于LLM安全分类的单层探针
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 用于LLM安全分类的单层探针
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 用于LLM安全分类的单层探针
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: The paper proposes PRISM, a lightweight single-layer logistic probe that separates and classifies text-level content danger and physical danger in LLM hidden states, achieving high accuracy with low FPR for LLM safety.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对大语言模型在具身智能体中可能产生物理危险的问题，该论文研究了文本内容危险与物理危险的分离性。基于此，提出了一种轻量级单层L2正则化逻辑回归探针PRISM，直接在LLM隐藏状态上分类内容危险与物理危险。实验表明，PRISM在SafeAgentBench上实现了86.2%-87.7%的准确率和极低的假阳性率，为LLM安全分类提供了一种参数极少的轻量级检测方法。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对大语言模型在具身智能体中可能产生物理危险的问题，该论文研究了文本内容危险与物理危险的分离性。基于此，提出了一种轻量级单层L2正则化逻辑回归探针PRISM，直接在LLM隐藏状态上分类内容危险与物理危险。实验表明，PRISM在SafeAgentBench上实现了86.2%-87.7%的准确率和极低的假阳性率，为LLM安全分类提供了一种参数极少的轻量级检测方法。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: lightweight small language model for content moderation and harmful content classification

quick_skim.title: Adaptive Model Compression (AMC): Saliency-Driven Resource Allocation for Ultra-Low-Power Transformer Inference
quick_skim.abstract: Deploying large-scale transformer models on resource-constrained edge devices remains a challenge due to the high energy and memory overhead inherent in static inference, which processes simple and complex tokens with uniform intensity. To address this, we propose Adaptive Model Compression (AMC), a saliency-driven framework that dynamically allocates hardware resources based on token importance. By implementing a multi-tier architecture, our system identifies critical high-saliency information for full-precision processing while aggressively reducing the rank and bit-width of less significant data. Experimental results demonstrate that AMC achieves a 59.2% reduction in system energy and a 2.24x increase in throughput on 45nm CMOS hardware. This approach effectively extends the battery life of mobile devices by utilizing high-definition compute only where necessary, maintaining robust performance with a marginal 3.6% accuracy trade-off.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Saliency-driven adaptive rank/bit-width compression for edge
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 面向边缘的显著性驱动自适应秩与位宽压缩
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 面向边缘的显著性驱动自适应秩与位宽压缩
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 面向边缘的显著性驱动自适应秩与位宽压缩
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: AMC proposes a saliency-driven adaptive model compression framework for ultra-low-power transformer inference on edge devices, dynamically reducing rank and bit-width for less important tokens, achieving 59.2% energy reduction and 2.24x throughput increase.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 在资源受限的边缘设备上部署大型Transformer模型时，静态推理对所有Token统一处理导致高能耗与内存开销。针对此问题，该论文提出自适应模型压缩框架AMC，基于Token重要性动态分配硬件资源，对高显著性信息保持全精度，对低显著性数据则大幅降低秩和位宽。实验表明，AMC实现了59.2%的系统能耗降低和2.24倍的吞吐量提升，为端侧高效推理提供了可复用的压缩方法。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 在资源受限的边缘设备上部署大型Transformer模型时，静态推理对所有Token统一处理导致高能耗与内存开销。针对此问题，该论文提出自适应模型压缩框架AMC，基于Token重要性动态分配硬件资源，对高显著性信息保持全精度，对低显著性数据则大幅降低秩和位宽。实验表明，AMC实现了59.2%的系统能耗降低和2.24倍的吞吐量提升，为端侧高效推理提供了可复用的压缩方法。

quick_skim.title: Reliability Scaling Laws for Quantized Large Language Models
quick_skim.abstract: Quantization is a powerful strategy to build capable and resource-efficient large language models (LLMs) by reducing the bitwidth of the parameters. While quantized LLMs achieve state-of-the-art performance on unperturbed inputs using standard predictive metrics, their performance on perturbed inputs, measured using reliability metrics, remains underexplored, despite its importance for reliable deployment. To address this gap, we first conduct a comprehensive reliability evaluation of quantized LLMs consisting of three key components: (1) Uncertainty: We assess the trustworthiness of LLMs quantized to 2, 3, 4, and 8 bits using six different quantization methods, employing established uncertainty metrics. (2) Calibration: We assess how well-calibrated the uncertainty estimates of quantized models are across model scales and bit precisions. (3) Robustness: We design character-level and word-level input perturbations to evaluate the reliability of quantized models under semantically-preserving variations in the inputs that arise in real-world applications. Second, we characterize how reliability scales with the total number of model bits. Our study reveals that while the performance scales monotonically with the total number of bits, the reliability scalings are nonlinear. A reliability peak occurs for 4-bit quantized models, indicating that quantizing moderately sized models offers the best reliability-efficiency trade-off. Additionally, our empirical findings reveal that quantization enhances the robustness of LLMs to natural input perturbations.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: quantized LLM reliability evaluation across bitwidths
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 跨位宽的量化LLM可靠性评估
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 跨位宽的量化LLM可靠性评估
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 跨位宽的量化LLM可靠性评估
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Quantized LLMs perform well on standard metrics but their reliability on perturbed inputs is underexplored. This paper comprehensively evaluates uncertainty, calibration, and robustness of LLMs quantized to 2-8 bits using six methods, revealing reliability scaling laws.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 量化LLM在标准指标上表现优异，但在扰动输入下的可靠性尚未充分探索。本文对2至8位量化的LLM进行了全面可靠性评估，涵盖不确定性、校准和鲁棒性三个维度，使用六种量化方法揭示了量化位宽与可靠性之间的缩放规律，为安全部署量化模型提供了重要参考。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 量化LLM在标准指标上表现优异，但在扰动输入下的可靠性尚未充分探索。本文对2至8位量化的LLM进行了全面可靠性评估，涵盖不确定性、校准和鲁棒性三个维度，使用六种量化方法揭示了量化位宽与可靠性之间的缩放规律，为安全部署量化模型提供了重要参考。

quick_skim.title: HyperSafe: Inference-Time Safety Recovery for Fine-Tuned Language Models
quick_skim.abstract: Safety alignment in large language models can be fragile under fine-tuning, as even benign task adaptation may increase harmful compliance. Existing defenses mainly follow two directions: they either intervene during or after fine-tuning through retraining or weight modification, which can be costly and may hurt task performance, or they use model-agnostic safety classifiers, which may miss failures specific to a given fine-tuned checkpoint. These limitations motivate a post hoc, model-specific, and non-invasive approach to safety restoration. To meet these requirements, we propose HyperSafe, a framework that restores safety behavior by generating a model-specific Safe Side Network (SSN) for each fine-tuned checkpoint. HyperSafe uses layer-wise activation fingerprints to capture how fine-tuning changes the model's inner representations. With a small set of given calibration prompts, the hypernetwork maps these fingerprints to the parameters of the \ssn{} in a single forward pass. The generated \ssn{} runs alongside the frozen fine-tuned model and performs prompt-level safety classification: harmful prompts are routed to refusal, while safe prompts are answered by the original fine-tuned model. Thus, HyperSafe requires no gradient updates, no safety data at deployment time, and no modification to the deployed model weights. We evaluate HyperSafe on two model families, Qwen2-7B and LLaMA-3-8B, across multiple safety benchmarks. HyperSafe reduces harmful response rates from 19-31% to below 1% on every held-out checkpoint, while keeping downstream task accuracy within 1% of the fine-tuned baseline on average. Code is available at https://github.com/nokronim/project-safety-remedy.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Inference-time safety recovery via lightweight Safe Side Network
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 推理时通过轻量安全侧网络恢复安全性
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 推理时通过轻量安全侧网络恢复安全性
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 推理时通过轻量安全侧网络恢复安全性
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: HyperSafe restores safety in fine-tuned LLMs at inference time by generating a lightweight, model-specific Safe Side Network (SSN) using layer-wise activation, offering a non-invasive alternative to costly retraining or generic classifiers.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 微调可能破坏大语言模型的安全对齐，现有防御方法要么重训练代价高，要么使用通用分类器缺乏针对性。本文提出HyperSafe框架，通过逐层激活生成模型特定的轻量安全侧网络（SSN），在推理时以非侵入方式恢复安全行为。该方法无需修改原模型权重，为微调模型的安全恢复提供了高效且针对性的解决方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 微调可能破坏大语言模型的安全对齐，现有防御方法要么重训练代价高，要么使用通用分类器缺乏针对性。本文提出HyperSafe框架，通过逐层激活生成模型特定的轻量安全侧网络（SSN），在推理时以非侵入方式恢复安全行为。该方法无需修改原模型权重，为微调模型的安全恢复提供了高效且针对性的解决方案。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: small parameter-efficient safety classifier for large language model prompts and responses

quick_skim.title: Transforming LLMs into Efficient Cross-Encoders via Knowledge Distillation for RAG Reranking
quick_skim.abstract: Cross-encoders achieve high reranking accuracy in Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipelines but impose quadratic inference costs that limit real-time deployment. We address this by fine-tuning LLaMA 3 (8B) as a drop-in reranker using a two-stage pipeline: supervised fine-tuning on a custom query-document relevance dataset via the Unsloth framework with LoRA adapters, followed by 4-bit quantization for efficient inference. The resulting model replaces the cross-encoder in a dual-retriever RAG pipeline combining BM25 and dense vector search. Evaluated on a domain-specific question-answering benchmark using the RAGAS framework, our fine-tuned LLaMA 3 reranker achieves gains of 14% in answer relevancy, 16% in context precision, 19% in answer similarity, and 21% in answer correctness over the cross-encoder baseline, while reducing inference overhead through 4-bit quantization. These results demonstrate that instruction-tuned LLMs can be adapted into accurate, efficient rerankers without the quadratic complexity of traditional cross-encoders.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: LLM distilled and 4-bit quantized into efficient cross-encoder
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: LLM蒸馏并4-bit量化为高效交叉编码器
quick_skim.llm_evidence: LLM蒸馏并4-bit量化为高效交叉编码器
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: LLM蒸馏并4-bit量化为高效交叉编码器
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Addresses high inference cost of cross-encoders in RAG by fine-tuning LLaMA 3 8B with LoRA and 4-bit quantization, achieving significant accuracy gains and efficient deployment as a drop-in reranker.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对RAG中交叉编码器推理成本高的问题，本文提出通过LoRA微调LLaMA 3 8B并结合4-bit量化，将其转化为高效重排器。实验表明，该模型在答案相关性和上下文精度上分别提升14%和16%，同时实现了高效推理部署，为LLM蒸馏与量化提供了有效实践。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对RAG中交叉编码器推理成本高的问题，本文提出通过LoRA微调LLaMA 3 8B并结合4-bit量化，将其转化为高效重排器。实验表明，该模型在答案相关性和上下文精度上分别提升14%和16%，同时实现了高效推理部署，为LLM蒸馏与量化提供了有效实践。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Find distilled, quantized, pruned, or parameter-efficient safety classifiers and guard models for harmful content detection

quick_skim.title: Data-Efficient Adaptation of LLMs via Attention Head Reweighting
quick_skim.abstract: Learning effectively from limited data is critical in domains like security where labeled examples are scarce. Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated some capabilities for data-efficient learning, especially through parameter-efficient adaptation methods, but continue to struggle when faced with few samples for difficult tasks. To meet this challenge, we propose Attention Head Reweighting (AHR), a data-efficient method that adapts LLMs to new text-classification tasks by learning only a single scalar per attention head. This drastically reduces the number of parameters that need to be learned by making use of the functional specialization of individual attention heads. Experiments on diverse open-source text classification datasets show that AHR can outperform standard baselines like LoRA when learning from limited samples, despite having 200-1000x fewer trainable parameters, as our AHR only modifies ~0.0001% of the model's parameters. In addition, our learned weights are easy to interpret and can be analyzed to better understand the mechanisms and attention heads responsible for in-context learning abilities in LLMs.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Parameter-efficient LLM adaptation for text classification via scalar reweighting
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过标量重加权实现LLM参数高效文本分类适配
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过标量重加权实现LLM参数高效文本分类适配
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过标量重加权实现LLM参数高效文本分类适配
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Attention Head Reweighting (AHR), a parameter-efficient method adapting LLMs to text classification by learning a single scalar per attention head, drastically reducing learnable parameters for data-efficient tasks.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对LLM在少样本文本分类任务上参数效率低的问题，本文提出注意力头重加权（AHR）方法。该方法利用注意力头的功能特化，仅学习每个头的单一标量，大幅减少需学习的参数量。实验表明，AHR在多种开源文本分类数据集上实现了高效适配，为安全等标注稀缺领域的轻量分类器构建提供了可行路径。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对LLM在少样本文本分类任务上参数效率低的问题，本文提出注意力头重加权（AHR）方法。该方法利用注意力头的功能特化，仅学习每个头的单一标量，大幅减少需学习的参数量。实验表明，AHR在多种开源文本分类数据集上实现了高效适配，为安全等标注稀缺领域的轻量分类器构建提供了可行路径。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: small parameter-efficient safety classifier for large language model prompts and responses
